title: FIX-467: Merge Conflict Resolution in helix-cli tickets/index.ts
description: This tour walks through the FIX-467 conflict resolution run for the helix-cli repository. A collateral merge conflict arose in src/tickets/index.ts when HLX-342 (CLI ticket subcommand improvements: help text, JSON output, and the new update-description subcommand) landed alongside a staging change. The conflict was already resolved before the agent ran — zero conflict markers remained in the file. The agent's job was to verify the resolution, confirm all 10 subcommands were preserved, and validate that the file compiles cleanly via tsc --noEmit. No source code was modified; all artifacts produced are verification and documentation records.

steps.description: The starting point of this run. The diagnosis phase confirmed that src/tickets/index.ts — the sole file listed in merge-conflicts.json as conflicted — had zero conflict markers remaining. A grep scan for <<<<<<<, =======, and >>>>>>> returned no matches. The 149-line file contained all 10 subcommands (list, latest, get, create, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle) from both the HLX-342 ticket branch and the staging change. Because the CLI has no involvement in library credential routing, this was a purely collateral conflict. The single success criterion (no markers remain) was already met, so no code changes were required.
steps.title: Overview: Diagnosis — Conflict Already Resolved

steps.description: The Diagnosis APL (Assertion-Plan-Log) records the single critical question: do any merge conflict markers remain in src/tickets/index.ts? The answer is definitively no — the grep scan returned zero matches. The evidence chain includes the grep scan result, the scout reference-map confirming 149 lines and 10 subcommands, and the explicit statement that this conflict was collateral from HLX-342 CLI improvements versus a staging change. The CLI has no library repo creation or GitHub API calls, so nothing beyond the conflict-marker check and typecheck is needed.
steps.title: Diagnosis APL: Evidence of Zero Conflict Markers

steps.description: The implementation plan codifies the zero-change strategy. Because the collateral conflict is already resolved, the plan prescribes exactly four steps: (1) npm install to ensure dependencies are present, (2) grep scan to formally verify zero conflict markers, (3) write a .env file with required CLI environment variables, and (4) run npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit) to confirm the resolved merge compiles correctly. No source files are to be modified. This plan acts as the authoritative record of what the agent is permitted to do and why — preventing accidental over-engineering of a conflict that required no further intervention.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: No Code Changes, Quality Gates Only

steps.description: The Implementation Plan APL answers two questions: is the collateral conflict resolved (yes — zero markers, 10 subcommands intact, HLX-342 and staging additions both preserved), and does the CLI have any library credential routing logic (no — it is purely a command-line tool for Helix API interaction with no GitHub operations or auth mode logic). The second answer is important because FIX-467 originated from a broader library credential change; the APL formally closes the loop by documenting that the CLI's conflict was purely incidental to that work.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: Confirming CLI Has No Library Logic

steps.description: The implementation record documents exactly what the agent did: npm install succeeded, a .env was written with HELIX_API_KEY and HELIX_URL, the grep scan confirmed zero conflict markers in tickets/index.ts, and npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit) exited 0 with no type errors. No files were modified. This artifact is the ground-truth record of CHK-01 (CLI TypeScript type checking passes) being satisfied. The table of verification commands and outcomes provides a clear audit trail showing the resolved merge is syntactically and type-correct.
steps.title: Implementation Actual: Steps Executed and Outcomes

steps.description: The Implementation APL closes the implementation phase with two confirmations: zero conflict markers remain in tickets/index.ts (all subcommands from both sides preserved), and tsc --noEmit exits 0 with no type errors. These two data points together prove that the merge resolution is both structurally complete (no dangling markers or dropped subcommands) and semantically valid (TypeScript is satisfied with the resulting code). The APL's concise statement — 'Collateral merge conflict in tickets/index.ts resolved. Typecheck passes. No library involvement in CLI.' — is the handoff signal to the code review phase.
steps.title: Implementation APL: Typecheck Passes, Conflict Resolved

steps.description: The code review phase independently verified the implementation agent's findings. The reviewer directly inspected src/tickets/index.ts (lines 4-13 for all 10 handler imports, lines 43-148 for all 10 switch cases with correct help text and ticket reference resolution). No missed requirements, correctness issues, regression risks, or code quality problems were found. The review explicitly notes that CHK-01 (CLI typecheck) remains valid and unaffected. No changes were made during review. This stop represents the final independent confirmation that the merge conflict resolution is correct and complete.
steps.title: Code Review: Independent Verification of the Resolution

steps.description: The Code Review APL is the final authoritative sign-off. It answers both review questions affirmatively: merge conflict markers are fully resolved (grep confirmed), and all CLI subcommands from both branches are preserved. The evidence explicitly cites the line ranges for imports (lines 4-13) and switch cases (lines 43-148), giving future readers precise locations to inspect if they want to validate the resolution themselves. The empty followups array confirms there are no deferred items — the run is complete and the helix-cli codebase is clean.
steps.title: Code Review APL: All 10 Subcommands Verified, No Issues
